verses.text: My heart's desire is Israel's salvation; they have 'a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.' Zeal misled. Earnestness without truth cannot save.
My heart's desire is Israel's salvation; they have 'a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.' Zeal misled. Earnestness without truth cannot save.
My heart's desire is Israel's salvation; they have 'a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.' Zeal misled. Earnestness without truth cannot save.
'Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.' Law fulfilled. He completes what the law could only point to.
'Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.' Law fulfilled. He completes what the law could only point to.
The word of faith is nigh, 'in thy mouth, and in thy heart.' Near at hand. Salvation is not far off but close to the heart.
The word of faith is nigh, 'in thy mouth, and in thy heart.' Near at hand. Salvation is not far off but close to the heart.
The word of faith is nigh, 'in thy mouth, and in thy heart.' Near at hand. Salvation is not far off but close to the heart.
'If thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and... believe in thine heart... thou shalt be saved.' Faith confessed. Heart-belief and open confession bring salvation.
'If thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and... believe in thine heart... thou shalt be saved.' Faith confessed. Heart-belief and open confession bring salvation.
'Whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed'; 'whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.' For all. Jew and Gentile alike are welcome.
'Whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed'; 'whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.' For all. Jew and Gentile alike are welcome.
'Whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed'; 'whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.' For all. Jew and Gentile alike are welcome.
How shall they call on Him unheard? 'how beautiful are the feet of them that preach!' Need of preaching. Faith comes by a sent messenger.
How shall they call on Him unheard? 'how beautiful are the feet of them that preach!' Need of preaching. Faith comes by a sent messenger.
Not all obeyed; 'faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.' Faith from the word. The gospel heard is the seed of faith.
Not all obeyed; 'faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.' Faith from the word. The gospel heard is the seed of faith.
They have heard; yet Israel is 'a disobedient and gainsaying people.' No excuse. Their unbelief is willful, not from ignorance.
